titleStmt.title: SII 25.251: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 251. (A.R. No. 251 of 1908.) JATANPĀL, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.

summary.p: On a slab from this village (now at Jagadalpūr).
This record is in corrupt Sanskrit prose engraved in the Nāgarī characters.
There are altogether eighteen lines but lines 16 and 17 are covered with the imprecatory figures of a pig and an ass.

On the top of this epigraph there are figures of the sun and the moon together with seven small circles in a row apparently representing the remaining seven planets. The significance of these is that the grant is to last as long as the navagrahas (the nine planets) endure, while the figures at the bottom represent a curse on the transgressor of the gift declaring his father, an ass and the mother, a pig.
It records the grant of land made by one Kāmā-nāyaka to one Rāhīlapāṁḍe. The mention of the mother Gaṁgādēvī, after the king's name is not clear.

The record does not furnish any other details of date except the Śaka year 1140 which corresponds to 1218 A.D. In this year there was an eclipse of the Sun and the month of Jyēshṭha was intercalary. It is so significant that it was doubly meritorious to make a gift.

Kāmā-nāyaka is mentioned as a chief (maṇḍalika) of Mahārāja Narasiṁhadēva. This king is known to have held the title Jagadēkabhūshaṇa as is evident from his Dantēwārā record (A.R.Ep., 1908, No. 245). He apparently belonged to the Nāgavaṁśa dynasty.
The names of Mēdani Pāṁḍe, Dēva-nāyaka, Jāmu Sāhu, Ghīkā Sēṭhi and Sōmāi are found mentioned as the protectors of this grant.
(Published in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. X, p. 42).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti sri māhārājā
narasiṁgha devaḥ rāvaḥ
gaṁgādevī mātā . maṁḍa
līka somarājaḥ . kā
mānāyakaḥ dataḥ bhu
mī rāhīla pāṁḍe pra
tigrāhī . medani pāṁḍa
devanāyaka jāmu sā
hu . ghīkā seḍhi somāi
Aite pālaka brahmavadha govadha
pitṛ vadha vālaghātaka . Aitanpāpe
ṣu līpyate . Asya bhumī pralo
paye . svadata ca pradāca vā johato va
suṁdharā ṣaṣṭhīvaruṣa sahasrā
ṇī narake jāyate krīmī
sākana 1140
Asya bhumīṁ pralopaya
gārdabha vāpa sukara mai .
Digital edition of SII 25.251 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
